title: Hot pausing containerized process to cut startup/init time
body:
/kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
One shot container images are slow, because they don't cut initialization and warmup times. Especially for things like Python. It would be much more beneficial if the image could include the process that is already loaded into memory and did some initialization work on its own. The process that waits for the next external io operation to proceed. External io operation is anything that reaches out of container - accessing a volume or the network.

comments.body:
Why not have the container loaded and running python in sleep mode, then exec into the container and run your python commands, would this satisfy your needs?

First build an image with python installed and call it python.

```
# cat /tmp/Containerfile 
from fedora
run dnf -y install python
# podman build -t python /tmp
```

Now create a daemonized container called quickpython using this python, and have the container just go to sleep.

```
# podman run -d --rm --name quickpython python  python -c "import signal; signal.pause()"
```

Now exec into the container and run python, this should be fast since the python is already loaded into memory.

```
# time podman exec quickpython python -c "print('this should be faster')"
this should be faster

real	0m0.210s
user	0m0.109s
sys	0m0.076s
```
To compare it to running just the python image directly.
```
# time podman run python python -c "print('this should be slower')"
this should be slower

real	0m0.353s
user	0m0.177s
sys	0m0.156s
```
It looks lt is twice as fast, for this simple example.  Your mileage may vary.

I think this is also possible right now with checkpoint/restore - you'd checkpoint-export a container with a Python VM set up and idle, and then restore as many copies as you want from that snapshot, having them come back up in a good state to begin working.
Need to see how it can speed up https://github.com/heroku/heroku-buildpack-python
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@abitrolly Have you tried any of these ideas?
I believe the suggested ideas are enough and have not heard a response.  Reopen if you have other ideas.

Checkpoint and restore require rootful Podman.
```
# podman container checkpoint

# podman container restore
```
The sleep idea could be expanded to lot's of containers, no need to run a single version of it. Just run  multiple sleeping containers as a pool.

The sleep idea works, because Python libs are already loaded in memory when container is sleeping. My idea is to pause and make snapshot of the initial process while it is waiting for I/O. The missing part is how to checkpoint at the right moment.
